## Summary

Detects whether two 64-bit integers have opposite signs.

## Contract

Returns true when exactly one input is negative, and false otherwise.

## Notes

This trick relies on the sign bit of x ^ y being set exactly when x and y have different sign bits.
It answers a sign-comparison question only. It does not compare magnitudes or arithmetic ordering.

## Optimized Implementation

```c
typedef struct {
    int64_t x;
    int64_t y;
} bh_input_t;
typedef bool bh_output_t;

bh_output_t bh_optimized_xor_signbit(bh_input_t input)
{
    return (input.x ^ input.y) < 0;
}
```

## Reference Implementation

```c
typedef struct {
    int64_t x;
    int64_t y;
} bh_input_t;
typedef bool bh_output_t;

bh_output_t bh_reference(bh_input_t input)
{
    return (input.x < 0) != (input.y < 0);
}
```
